current.summary: Hooks spec modularization design complete; panel review complete with 4 consensus findings; ready for planning phase

activeWork.description: Modularizing hooks.md into separate documents with reference implementations using W3C-style structure
activeWork.progress: Design doc written and locked. Panel review (skeptic, early adopter, spec author) completed with synthesis. Consensus on: (1) conformance section needs inlined minimums, (2) test vectors need format documentation, (3) cross-reference scope is larger, (4) CHANGELOG ownership needs clarity. One design disagreement resolved: bundling modularization + ref impls together. Ready to move to planning phase with task breakdown and dependencies.

git.lastCommit: 928449d bd sync: 2026-04-08 conflict detection complete + handoff

recentDecisions: Keep hooks.md as core spec (don't rename to core.md; preserves existing links)
Use relative links with inline context for cross-references (modern pattern, not numbered refs)
Structure: hooks.md (core) + events.md + tools.md + capabilities.md + test-vectors/README.md + README.md (directory index)
Inline conformance minimums in core spec (don't just link to extracted docs)
Document test vector format with pairing rules in test-vectors/README.md
Bundle modularization + reference implementations (single feature, not split)
Real Python + TypeScript reference implementations (no pseudocode)
Add comprehensive CHANGELOG entry documenting all section mappings and renumbering
nextSteps: Planning phase: break work into 10-15 tasks with dependencies (spec restructuring, ref impls, CI setup, docs)
Address panel consensus findings during planning (conformance inlining, test vectors doc, cross-reference scope, CHANGELOG)
Execution phase: implement modularization task-by-task, starting with core spec restructuring
Validation: verify all cross-references work, test vectors are consumable by ref impls, README clarity for new contributors

context.notes: Design doc in docs/plans/2026-04-08-hooks-spec-modularization-design.md covers all 10 design decisions with detailed rationale. Panel review findings: Skeptic identified gaps (test vectors format, adapter choice rationale, cross-reference scope); Early Adopter evaluated onboarding experience (README clarity, reference impl accessibility, directory structure); Spec Author checked normative boundaries and versioning coherence. Consensus on 4 major findings. One disagreement: Skeptic recommended splitting modularization from ref impls to ship faster; design author (Maive) preferred bundling for coherent onboarding experience and stronger story. Resolved in favor of bundling after discussion of tradeoffs. Develop state file updated. When resuming: start planning phase with brainstorm or plan skill to task-break the implementation work.

previousSessions.summary: Pragmatic review of hooks.md spec complete; confirmed modular structure needed; locked in on real code reference implementations (no pseudocode), W3C-style document split
completedWork: Reviewed hooks.md spec for pragmatic improvements and maintainability
Identified monolithic 900-line structure as core problem
Confirmed W3C-style modularization (separate spec + registries/data) best pattern
Locked in on real Python + TypeScript reference implementations (rejected pseudocode)
Determined registries (§7-10) are data with independent lifecycle
Decided test vectors stay as JSON files with documentation
nextSteps: Create design doc with complete file plan and cross-reference strategy
Run panel review from three perspectives (skeptic, adopter, spec author)
Move to planning phase with task breakdown
